title: Add security/architecture rationale annotation pattern
body:
## Summary

Create a standardized annotation pattern that allows developers to document security/architecture rationales in a way the reviewer can recognize and respect.

## Proposed Patterns

### Security Rationale
```go
// @security-rationale: auth-delegation
// Authorization delegated to GitHub API. Token permissions are authoritative.
func handlePostFindings(...) {
```

### Architecture Rationale  
```go
// @arch-rationale: fail-at-boundary
// GitHub API validates owner/repo format. Duplicate validation would be redundant.
if input.Owner == "" {
```

### Known Limitation
```go
// @known-limitation: issue#187
// Full diff fetch is required for position mapping. Optimization tracked.
diff, err := s.deps.RemoteGitHubClient.GetPRDiff(...)
```

## Reviewer Integration

1. Update reviewer prompts to recognize these annotations
2. When a rationale annotation is present, the reviewer should:
   - Acknowledge the documented decision
   - Only flag if the annotation seems incorrect or outdated
   - Reference the annotation in any related finding

## Benefits

- Self-documenting code decisions
- Reduces repeated findings on intentional patterns
- Creates searchable/auditable design decision log
- Low friction - just a comment pattern

## Alternative: Structured Annotations

Could use structured format for machine parsing:
```go
// @rationale(type=security, id=auth-delegation, issue=#184)
// Authorization delegated to GitHub API.
```

## Priority

P2 - Low effort to implement, requires discipline to adopt
